name: Jeremy McNichols
headline: Efficient depth back buried in a crowded Washington room
body:
McNichols quietly delivered on his limited 2025 touches, averaging 5.02 yards per carry on 44 attempts and breaking off a 60-yard run that doubled as his only rushing score. He shed contact at a 3.48 yards-after-contact clip and added 196 receiving yards on 25 grabs, posting a tidy 7.2% target share across a 31.9% snap rate. The problem isn't the production rate; it's the volume. Sixteen games yielded just 47.7 fantasy points, and that ceiling shrinks further in 2026.

Washington imported Rachaad White and Jerome Ford this offseason, stacking the depth chart and pushing McNichols firmly into a contingency role. Season-long projections in the neighborhood of 17 carries and 7 catches confirm he won't sniff standalone value barring an injury ahead of him. He's a deep-league handcuff and dynasty afterthought, useful only if the backfield thins out and his proven efficiency finally gets a runway.
